steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Command of Evidence (Textual) question asking why Rafael accompanies his brother. The answer must be grounded directly in what the passage states.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Rafael has not received formal instruction in poetry and wants to ask the sisters' famous father to be his mentor.: The passage never says Rafael lacks instruction or that he wants a mentor. ✗
  • Choice B — Rafael has learned all his brother can teach him and now desires to be taught by the sisters.: It is the narrator, not Rafael, who has learned all her tutor can teach. The passage says nothing about Rafael wanting instruction from the sisters. ✗
  • Choice C — Rafael hopes to recite his own poetry to the sisters and solicit their comments on his work.: The passage never mentions Rafael wanting to recite or share his work. ✗
  • Choice D — Rafael anticipates having the opportunity to be introduced to both the sisters and their father.: The passage states directly that Rafael "was hoping not only to meet us but to make the acquaintance of the poet himself at Mamá's house", which captures both goals — meeting the sisters and meeting their poet father. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
